description_long: This is a tall, slender ceramic vase with a flared mouth and a single curved handle attached to its neck and shoulder. The body is decorated with intricate black-figure artwork, likely inspired by ancient Greek pottery, depicting human and possibly mythological figures in dynamic poses. The upper shoulder features a repeating leaf or petal motif encircling the neck, while the main body is adorned with detailed narrative scenes. The vase sits on a small, rounded foot that provides stability. The surface shows a combination of matte and slightly glossy finishes, with some areas appearing worn or faded. The overall color palette consists of earthy terracotta tones contrasted with deep black painted designs.
description: A tall, slender terracotta vase with a flared mouth, single handle, and intricate black-figure artwork depicting figures and decorative motifs.
description_view_0: Front view showing the flared mouth, detailed black-figure artwork, and the main narrative scene on the body.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the single curved handle and the continuation of the decorative motifs around the neck and body.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the handle attachment and a less decorated, more plain terracotta surface.
description_view_3: Angled view showing both the handle and a secondary scene or figure, with the foot and mouth clearly visible.
attribute_pattern: narrative black-figure scenes with geometric border
